note: CONFIRMED #6121 (rocicorp/mono d4f33d6a6, lands AFTER the @rocicorp/zero 1.7.0 harness pin). A start cursor anchored on a NULL-sorted row returns EMPTY on the stock zero-cache 1.7.0 reference: server-side sqlite table-source compiles `col > NULL` (SQL NULL, matches nothing), so the forward continuation through the NULL group came back empty. Found by the metamorphic startSuffix relation on a SINGLE target (no oracle). The stock-vs-orez differential MISSES this because the sweep generator has no nullable-column start-cursor axis; had it emitted one, stock ([]) and orez-local (correct rows) would DIVERGE and the differential would also flag it. orez-local PASSES this shape (it ships full snapshots and materializes client-side, so it never pushes the start into the buggy sqlite fetch). Classified known-gap, NON-GATING; flips green once the harness pin advances past mono d4f33d6a6.
invariant: materialize(spec).rows must equal materialize(reference).rows sliced strictly after the row with id 't1' (exclusive start). t1 is the first NULL-dueAt row (NULLs sort first ascending), so the correct result is the entire remaining ordered suffix.
sourcesNote: Sources are regenerated deterministically from harness/src/fixture-data.ts SEED (seed=1). Replay re-runs the lane, which regenerates the fixture and the spec.
replay: cd harness && mise x node@22 -- bun src/metamorphic-lane.ts --against stock-zero
prerequisites: the stock-zero target spawns zero-cache under Node; @rocicorp/zero-sqlite3 supports Node 22.x/24.x (NOT 25.x), so run under a supported Node: `mise x node@22 -- ...` (or any Node 22/24 on PATH)

the @rocicorp/zero-sqlite3 native binding must be built: from harness/, `cd node_modules/@rocicorp/zero-sqlite3 && mise x node@22 -- npm run install` produces build/Release/better_sqlite3.node (prebuild-install, node-gyp fallback)
embedded-postgres darwin/linux dylib sonames are recreated automatically at boot by harness/src/targets/stock-zero.ts (no manual step)
verified fresh-environment on 2026-07-11: this exact command reproduced base=[] vs a 46-row expected suffix deterministically (two runs).
upstreamFix: rocicorp/mono d4f33d6a6 (#6121) — compile NULL cursor bounds into null-safe start constraints
orezRustCoverage: crates/sync-core/tests/query_ast.rs::start_cursor_null_ordering — Orez's Rust server compiler already handles NULL cursors correctly (server-side); this gap is the stock 1.7 reference path, not Orez's own engine.
